[1614]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 亟返逆旅 - Hastily returned to the inn, indicating urgency.
  • 紧候开舟 - Waiting anxiously for the boat to depart.
  • 乃绐曰 - Then deceived them by saying, where '绐' means to deceive.
  • 昨得家书，促回甚急，必有他故，不得同行矣 - I received a letter from home yesterday, urging me to return urgently; there must be some other reason, so I cannot travel with you.
  • 二生信之，执手而别 - The two scholars believed him, and they parted ways, holding hands.
  • 韶是晚再去，金雁已先在矣 - Shao went back that evening, and Jin Yan was already there.
  • 遂导过亭北竹阴中，半里余 - He then led her to the north side of the pavilion under the bamboo shade, about half a li away.
  • 朱门素壁，灯烛交辉 - Red doors and white walls, with lights and candles shining brightly.
  • 才及重堂，丽人迎笑 - Just as they reached the grand hall, the beautiful woman greeted them with a smile.
  • 此吾主所御，今以劝郎，意亦不薄矣 - This cup is used by my master, and now I offer it to you, which shows no small favor.
  • 宿留月余，不啻胶漆 - They stayed for over a month, as inseparable as glue and lacquer.
  • 一夕，丽人语韶曰 - One night, the beautiful woman said to Shao.
  • 妾死时伪汉方盛，主宠复深 - When I died, the false Han was at its height, and my master's favor was deep.
  • 玉匣珠襦，殡送极一时之富贵 - With a jade box and pearl robe, the burial was extremely wealthy and grand.
  • 幽宫神道，坟茔备一品之威仪 - The tomb and the path to the afterlife were equipped with the dignity of the first rank.
  • 是致五体依然，三魂不昧 - Thus, my five senses remain intact, and my three souls are not obscured.
  • 向者庐君爱女南极夫人偶此嬉游，授妾以太阴炼形之术 - Formerly, Lady Nanji, the beloved daughter of Lord Lu, happened to play here and taught me the technique of the moon's essence to refine my form.
  • 为之既久，不异生人 - After practicing it for a long time, I am no different from a living person.
  • 夜出昼藏，逍遥自在 - I emerge at night and hide during the day, free and at ease.
  • 君宜就市求青羊乳半杯，勤勤滴妾目中，乳尽眼开，白日可起 - You should go to the market and get half a cup of blue sheep's milk, and drop it into my eyes drop by drop. When the milk runs out, my eyes will open, and I can rise during the day.
  • 韶如言求乳，以滴其两 - Shao followed her instructions and got the milk, dripping it into her eyes.
  • 屈指三旬，然能步 - After counting three weeks, she could walk.
  • 或同携素手，游衍隧中，或并倚香肩，笑歌亭上 - Sometimes she held his hand and wandered through the tunnel, sometimes she leaned on his fragrant shoulder and sang and laughed on the pavilion.
  • 韶迷恋情深，乡闾念浅，春来秋去，四载于兹 - Shao was deeply infatuated and had shallow thoughts of his hometown; four years have passed since spring to autumn.
  • 虽比目并游之鳞，戢翼双栖之羽，未足以喻其绸缪婉恋也 - Even the paired fish swimming together, the birds with folded wings resting together, cannot adequately describe their affectionate and tender love.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 伪汉 - Refers to the period when the Han dynasty was in decline or under foreign rule, often used to describe a time of political instability.
  • 南极夫人 - Refers to a noblewoman, possibly a deity or a highly respected figure, associated with the southern pole star, symbolizing guidance and protection.
  • 太阴炼形之术 - Refers to a Taoist practice of refining one's body using the essence of the moon, a mystical and spiritual technique.
  • 青羊乳 - Blue sheep's milk, a rare and valuable substance in Chinese mythology and folklore, often associated with immortality or rejuvenation.
  • 玉匣珠襦 - Refers to a jade box and pearl robe, symbols of wealth and luxury in ancient Chinese funerary practices.
  • 幽宫神道 - Refers to the tomb and the spiritual path leading to the afterlife, emphasizing the grandeur and solemnity of the burial.

Proper Nouns

  • 韶 - The name of the male protagonist.
  • 金雁 - The name of the beautiful woman.
  • 陈、梁二生 - The names of the two scholars.
  • 庐君 - The name of the lord or nobleman.
  • 南极夫人 - The name of the noblewoman or deity.
  • 伪汉 - A period of political instability.
  • 太阴 - The moon, often associated with feminine and mystical qualities in Chinese culture.
  • 青羊 - Blue sheep, a mythical creature often associated with immortality.
  • 玉匣 - A jade box, a symbol of wealth and luxury.
  • 珠襦 - A pearl robe, another symbol of wealth and luxury.
  • 幽宫 - The tomb, a place of rest and transition.
  • 神道 - The spiritual path leading to the afterlife.
  • 一品 - The highest rank in the Chinese imperial bureaucracy.

[1615]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 是年冬初 - Literally 'in the early winter of that year,' introduces the time frame.
  • 无故忽耳 - Ambiguous phrase likely meaning 'without reason, suddenly' or 'for no apparent reason.'
  • 凄惶感怆 - Means 'deeply sad and distressed,' describes the emotional state.
  • 自缢于隧间 - Literally 'to hang oneself in the tunnel,' indicates an attempt at suicide.
  • 阴寿未终 - Means 'the yin life span has not yet ended,' refers to the afterlife or spiritual existence.
  • 阴质未化 - Means 'the yin essence has not yet transformed,' suggests a state of spiritual or physical transformation.
  • 沉溺世缘 - Means 'to be deeply entangled in worldly affairs,' indicates a strong attachment to the material world.
  • 致君非命 - Means 'to cause the lord to have an untimely death,' suggests a fatal outcome.
  • 冥司必加重谴 - Means 'the underworld officials will surely impose a heavier punishment,' refers to divine retribution.
  • 彼此牵缠 - Means 'to be entangled with each other,' indicates a mutual dependency or entanglement.
  • 何时是了 - Means 'when will it all end?' expresses a sense of hopelessness.
  • 定数 - Means 'fate' or 'destiny,' suggests an inescapable force.
  • 纵曰舍生 - Means 'even if one says to give up life,' indicates a hypothetical scenario.
  • 亦为徒死 - Means 'it will also be in vain,' suggests that death would be meaningless.
  • 金雁、钿蝉辈 - Refers to a group of items, likely jewelry or ornaments.
  • 依依不忍舍 - Means 'to be reluctant to part,' expresses a strong emotional attachment.
  • 设饮食 - Means 'to prepare food and drink,' indicates a farewell gesture.
  • 与韶送程 - Means 'to accompany Shao on his journey,' suggests a farewell.
  • 赤金条脱一双 - Means 'a pair of gold bracelets,' refers to a specific item of jewelry.
  • 明珠步摇一对 - Means 'a pair of pearl hair ornaments,' refers to another specific item of jewelry.
  • 表诚寓意 - Means 'to express sincerity and meaning,' indicates the symbolic value of the gifts.
  • 睹物思人 - Means 'to think of someone upon seeing an object,' expresses a sentiment of remembrance.
  • 再会无期 - Means 'we will never meet again,' indicates a permanent farewell.
  • 愿郎珍重 - Means 'I hope you take care,' expresses a wish for well-being.
  • 掩袂障面而还 - Means 'to cover the face with a sleeve and return,' indicates a gesture of sorrow.
  • 悲不自己 - Means 'to be unable to control one's sadness,' expresses intense grief.
  • 残泪盈眶 - Means 'tears welling up in the eyes,' describes the physical manifestation of sadness.
  • 顾盼之间，失其所在 - Means 'in the blink of an eye, she disappeared,' indicates a sudden and unexpected departure.
  • 重寻原店，收拾归家 - Means 'to search for the original store and gather to return home,' indicates a final action before departure.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 丽人 - Refers to a beautiful woman, likely a supernatural or spiritual being.
  • 韶 - Likely a male character, possibly a scholar or a noble.
  • 金雁、钿蝉 - Refers to specific types of jewelry, likely symbolic of status or affection.
  • 赤金条脱 - A type of gold bracelet, likely a valuable gift.
  • 明珠步摇 - A type of pearl hair ornament, likely a valuable gift.
  • 冥司 - Refers to the underworld officials, indicating a belief in divine retribution.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The sentence '是年冬初，丽人无故忽耳' is elliptical and requires interpretation of the phrase '无故忽耳.'
  • The sentence '凄惶感怆，欲自缢于隧间' has a straightforward structure but requires understanding of the emotional state and the action.
  • The parallel structure '阴寿未终，阴质未化' requires understanding of the spiritual concepts.
  • The cause-and-effect structure '沉溺世缘，致君非命' requires understanding of the relationship between worldly attachment and fatal outcomes.
  • The parallel structure '冥司必加重谴，彼此牵缠，何时是了' requires understanding of the consequences and the sense of hopelessness.
  • The parallel structure '定数，举莫能逃' requires understanding of the concept of fate.
  • The hypothetical structure '纵曰舍生，亦为徒死' requires understanding of the conditional scenario.
  • The parallel structure '金雁、钿蝉辈，依依不忍舍' requires understanding of the emotional attachment to the items.
  • The parallel structure '设饮食，与韶送程' requires understanding of the farewell gestures.
  • The parallel structure '赤金条脱一双，明珠步摇一对' requires understanding of the gifts and their symbolic value.
  • The parallel structure '表诚寓意，睹物思人' requires understanding of the symbolic value of the gifts.
  • The parallel structure '再会无期，愿郎珍重' requires understanding of the farewell and the wish for well-being.
  • The parallel structure '掩袂障面而还，悲不自己' requires understanding of the gesture of sorrow and the emotional state.
  • The parallel structure '残泪盈眶，顾盼之间，失其所在' requires understanding of the physical manifestation of sadness and the sudden disappearance.
  • The parallel structure '重寻原店，收拾归家' requires understanding of the final action before departure.

Proper Nouns

  • 丽人 - The name of the female character.
  • 韶 - The name of the male character.
  • 金雁 - A type of jewelry.
  • 钿蝉 - A type of jewelry.
  • 赤金条脱 - A specific type of gold bracelet.
  • 明珠步摇 - A specific type of pearl hair ornament.
  • 冥司 - The underworld officials.
  • 阴寿 - The yin life span.
  • 阴质 - The yin essence.

[1616]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 数月 - Time phrase meaning 'several months.'
  • 梁生至自襄阳 - Classical construction meaning 'Mr. Liang arrived from Xiangyang.'
  • 陈生客死房县 - Phrase meaning 'Mr. Chen died as a guest in Fang County.'
  • 方咎韶负约 - Phrase meaning 'Fang Jiu Shao broke the agreement.'
  • 韶密以告 - Phrase meaning 'Shao secretly informed.'
  • 弗信也 - Phrase meaning 'did not believe it.'
  • 出条脱步摇示之 - Phrase meaning 'showed the jade bracelets and hair ornaments to him.'
  • 乃惊曰 - Phrase meaning 'then exclaimed.'
  • 此非尘上间物 - Phrase meaning 'these are not ordinary things.'
  • 奇宝也 - Phrase meaning 'they are treasures.'
  • 诚子之遇仙矣 - Phrase meaning 'truly you have met a fairy.'
  • 知此事者 - Phrase meaning 'those who know this matter.'
  • 惟梁生一人 - Phrase meaning 'only Mr. Liang.'
  • 故生有《琵琶佳遇》诗 - Phrase meaning 'therefore, the author composed a poem titled 'Encounter with the Lute.''
  • 并附于此 - Phrase meaning 'and it is attached here.'
  • 诗云 - Phrase meaning 'the poem says.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 襄阳 - Refers to Xiangyang, a city in Hubei Province.
  • 房县 - Refers to Fang County, also in Hubei Province.
  • 条脱步摇 - Refers to traditional Chinese jewelry, specifically jade bracelets and hair ornaments.
  • 尘上间物 - Phrase meaning 'ordinary things' or 'mundane objects.'
  • 奇宝 - Refers to rare and valuable treasures.
  • 遇仙 - Refers to encountering a fairy or supernatural being, a common theme in Chinese folklore.
  • 《琵琶佳遇》 - The title of the poem, which suggests a romantic or mystical encounter involving a lute.

Proper Nouns

  • 梁生 - Refers to Mr. Liang, a character in the story.
  • 陈生 - Refers to Mr. Chen, another character in the story.
  • 方咎韶 - Refers to Fang Jiu Shao, a person who broke the agreement.
  • 韶 - Refers to Shao, who informed about the situation.
  • 《琵琶佳遇》 - The title of the poem.

[1617]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 追随钿雁双娇娆 - Poetic phrase describing a beautiful woman or couple, likely referring to a romantic or idealized relationship.
  • 直入金屏最深处 - Literally 'directly entering the deepest part of the golden screen,' which may symbolize entering a private or intimate space.
  • 春风东来绽牡丹 - Metaphor for the arrival of spring and the blooming of peonies, often used to symbolize beauty or renewal.
  • 洞房香雾椒兰 - Describes a fragrant and luxurious bedroom, with '椒兰' referring to spices and incense.
  • 合情惯作云雨梦 - Euphemism for sexual intimacy, using the metaphor of clouds and rain.
  • 鸳枕生愁清夜阑 - Describes a night filled with sorrow while sharing a bed, with '鸳枕' referring to a couple's shared pillow.
  • 前朝佳丽夸环燕 - Refers to the beauty of past imperial concubines, with '环燕' possibly symbolizing a group of beautiful women.
  • 图出千人万人羡 - Means that the beauty of these women is admired by thousands or even tens of thousands of people.
  • 太真颜色赵肌肤 - Describes the beauty of Yang Guifei, a famous Tang Dynasty concubine, with '赵肌肤' referring to her fair and smooth skin.
  • 绣帐悬灯几回见 - Describes the luxurious and romantic setting of a bedchamber with embroidered curtains and hanging lamps.
  • 情缘忽断两分飞 - Means that a love relationship suddenly ends, and the couple is separated.
  • 归来如梦还如痴 - Describes the feeling of returning after a separation, as if in a dream or in a state of confusion.
  • 缥囊留得万金赠 - Refers to a silk pouch containing a valuable gift, possibly a symbol of love or wealth.
  • 凄凉忍看徒伤悲 - Describes a feeling of loneliness and sorrow, with '徒伤悲' meaning 'only to cause sadness.'
  • 徒伤悲，难再得 - Emphasizes the difficulty of recovering from such sorrow and the impossibility of regaining what was lost.
  • 当初若悟有分离，此生何用逢倾国 - Reflective statement suggesting that if one had realized the possibility of separation earlier, it would have been better not to have met someone so beautiful or influential.
  • 韶从此不复再娶 - Means that the person named 'Shao' decides not to remarry after this experience.
  • 投礼道士周玄初为师，授五雷斩勘之法 - Describes Shao becoming a disciple of Daoist master Zhou Xuanchu, who teaches him the 'Five Thunder Slash Examination' technique, a form of Daoist ritual or martial art.
  • 往来两浙间，驱邪治病，祷雨祈晴，多有应验 - Describes Shao's activities in the Zhejiang region, where he performs rituals to exorcise evil, heal diseases, and pray for rain or clear skies, with many successful outcomes.
  • 后失所在，近有人于终南及嵩山诸处见之，疑其得道云 - States that Shao later disappears, but some people have reportedly seen him in the areas around Mount Zhongnan and Mount Song, suggesting he may have attained enlightenment or immortality.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 钿雁 - A poetic term often used to describe a beautiful woman or a couple.
  • 金屏 - Refers to a luxurious screen or partition, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a wealthy or elegant setting.
  • 牡丹 - The peony, a symbol of beauty and wealth in Chinese culture.
  • 椒兰 - Spices and incense, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe a fragrant and luxurious environment.
  • 云雨梦 - A euphemism for sexual intimacy, derived from the metaphor of clouds and rain.
  • 鸳枕 - A shared pillow, symbolizing a couple's intimacy.
  • 环燕 - A group of beautiful women, often used in classical Chinese literature to describe the beauty of imperial concubines.
  • 太真 - A nickname for Yang Guifei, one of the most famous concubines in Chinese history.
  • 五雷斩勘之法 - A Daoist ritual or martial art technique, often associated with exorcism and spiritual power.
  • 两浙 - Refers to the Zhejiang region in China, historically known for its cultural and economic significance.
  • 终南及嵩山 - Mount Zhongnan and Mount Song, both famous Daoist mountains in China, often associated with spiritual enlightenment.
  • 辽东 - Refers to the Liaodong region in northeastern China, historically known for its strategic importance.
  • 清河太守 - The governor of Qinghe Commandery, a historical administrative division in China.
  • 南郡 - Refers to the Nan Commandery, a historical administrative division in China.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • This line describes a romantic or idealized relationship, with the phrase '直入金屏最深处' possibly symbolizing entering a private or intimate space.
  • This line uses natural imagery to describe a luxurious and romantic setting, with '洞房香雾椒兰' emphasizing the fragrant and opulent environment.
  • This line describes a night filled with both intimacy and sorrow, with '鸳枕生愁清夜阑' emphasizing the emotional complexity of the scene.
  • This line contrasts the beauty of past imperial concubines with the admiration they received, with '图出千人万人羡' emphasizing their widespread appeal.
  • This line describes the beauty of Yang Guifei, with '绣帐悬灯几回见' emphasizing the luxurious and romantic setting.
  • This line describes the sudden end of a love relationship and the emotional turmoil that follows, with '归来如梦还如痴' emphasizing the dreamlike and confused state of mind.
  • This line describes the emotional impact of losing a loved one, with '凄凉忍看徒伤悲' emphasizing the sorrow and loneliness.
  • This line emphasizes the difficulty of recovering from such sorrow and the impossibility of regaining what was lost.
  • This line reflects on the possibility of separation and the regret of having met someone so beautiful or influential.
  • This line describes Shao's decision not to remarry and his subsequent journey into Daoism, with '授五雷斩勘之法' emphasizing his spiritual transformation.
  • This line describes Shao's activities in the Zhejiang region, with '往来两浙间，驱邪治病，祷雨祈晴，多有应验' emphasizing his successful rituals.

[1618]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 淳熙十二年 - The year 1185 CE during the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 赵宗室叔侄三人 - Refers to three male relatives of the imperial clan of Zhao, specifically a nephew and two uncles.
  • 调选 - The process of being selected for official positions.
  • 共买小舟 - Means 'they jointly bought a small boat.'
  • 经吴兴 - Means 'passed through Wuxing (a place name).'
  • 过溪中一滩 - Means 'crossed a stream with a shallow area.'
  • 午风大作 - Means 'a strong wind arose at noon.'
  • 天色晦瞑 - Means 'the sky became dark and gloomy.'
  • 舟即沦覆 - Means 'the boat immediately capsized.'
  • 水浅不死 - Means 'the water was shallow, so they did not drown.'
  • 适一笼漂至 - Means 'just then a basket floated to them.'
  • 叔之诰敕袍靴之属也 - Means 'the uncle's imperial edicts, robes, and boots.'
  • 继又漂至 - Means 'then another basket floated to them.'
  • 二侄文书在焉 - Means 'the two nephews' documents were in it.'
  • 日已暮 - Means 'the sun had already set.'
  • 投宿村舍 - Means 'they stayed overnight in a village hut.'
  • 清晨徒步而行 - Means 'in the early morning, they walked on foot.'
  • 见田父荷锄治地 - Means 'they saw a farmer carrying a hoe and tending the land.'
  • 以昨事告之 - Means 'they told him about yesterday's events.'
  • 何不问赵法师也 - Means 'Why don't you ask Zhao the priest?'
  • 三赵行访 - Means 'the three Zhao relatives went to visit.'
  • 法师亦宗室素相善者 - Means 'the priest was also a close friend of the imperial clan.'
  • 彼小小川渎 - Means 'those small streams and ditches.'
  • 何能坏舟 - Means 'how could they capsize a boat?'
  • 是必有异 - Means 'there must be something unusual.'
  • 吾术制神鬼 - Means 'my methods can control spirits and ghosts.'
  • 立可知矣 - Means 'I can find out immediately.'
  • 少顷 - Means 'a short while later.'
  • 神鬼盈门 - Means 'spirits and ghosts filled the room.'
  • 询昨为祟者 - Means 'asked about the spirit that caused the disaster yesterday.'
  • 即此田父也 - Means 'it was this farmer.'
  • 法师责之 - Means 'the priest scolded him.'
  • 索舟中之物 - Means 'asked for the items in the boat.'
  • 一一皆分属鬼家矣 - Means 'all of them have already been divided among the spirits.'
  • 某忝为当界土地 - Means 'I am ashamed to be the local land god.'
  • 前此奉城隍司牒 - Means 'earlier, I received an order from the City God's office.'
  • 命覆此舟 - Means 'to inspect this boat.'
  • 舟中物皆据牒交领 - Means 'all the items in the boat were handed over according to the order.'
  • 惟三人诰命书制 - Means 'only the three people's imperial edicts and documents.'
  • 非籍中所载 - Means 'were not listed in the records.'
  • 旋送还之矣 - Means 'they were quickly returned to us.'
  • 牒存可验 - Means 'the order can be verified.'

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 淳熙十二年 - The year 1185 CE during the reign of Emperor Xiaozong of Song.
  • 赵宗室 - Refers to the imperial clan of Zhao, which ruled the Song dynasty.
  • 调选 - The process of selecting officials for government positions, a common practice in imperial China.
  • 吴兴 - A historical county in present-day Zhejiang Province, known for its cultural significance.
  • 城隍司 - The office responsible for managing the local city god, a deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 城隍 - The city god, a protective deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 土地 - The local land god, a deity responsible for the welfare of a particular area.

Proper Nouns

  • 淳熙 - The era name of Emperor Xiaozong of Song (1163-1189).
  • 临安 - The capital of the Southern Song dynasty, present-day Hangzhou.
  • 吴兴 - A historical county in present-day Zhejiang Province.
  • 城隍司 - The office responsible for managing the local city god.
  • 城隍 - The city god, a protective deity in Chinese folk religion.
  • 土地 - The local land god, a deity responsible for the welfare of a particular area.
  • 赵法师 - The priest mentioned in the story, a member of the imperial clan.

[1619]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 嬉戏相得 - Playing together and getting along well.
  • 以尔为次夫人 - I will make you my secondary wife.
  • 遗精矣 - He had an emission of semen.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 道者 - Taoist priest or practitioner.
  • 修炼 - Taoist practices of cultivation and self-improvement.
  • 阳寿 - Predetermined lifespan according to fate.

Proper Nouns

  • 张余庆 - Zhang Yuqing (a fictional character).
  • 王某 - Wang (the name of the servant).
  • 道者 - Taoist priest (a title or role).
  • 小娘子 - Young lady (a term of respect for a young woman).

[1620]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 自缢 - Euphemism for suicide by hanging.
  • 表其阍 - To erect a tablet at the gate to honor someone's virtue or deeds.
  • 旋风大作 - A whirlwind suddenly arose, describing a sudden and violent storm.
  • 甘人唾骂 - To willingly face people's scorn and abuse, indicating a willingness to endure criticism.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 高邮 - A city in Jiangsu Province, China, known for its historical significance.
  • 张同知 - A title for a magistrate, indicating Zhang's official position.
  • 王氏女 - Refers to a woman from the Wang family, a common surname in China.
  • 天仙织女 - A reference to the Chinese mythological figure of the Weaver Girl, who is often depicted as a celestial weaver.
  • 天曹 - A concept in Chinese mythology referring to the divine bureaucracy, where gods and spirits are believed to be governed.

Proper Nouns

  • 高邮 - A place name, indicating the location of the story.
  • 张同知 - A personal name and title, indicating the main character's identity and position.
  • 王氏女 - A personal name, indicating the identity of the woman involved.
  • 来仪 - A personal name, indicating the identity of Zhang's servant.
  • 天仙织女 - A mythological figure, indicating the identity of the divine figure.
  • 天曹 - A mythological concept, indicating the divine bureaucracy.

[1621]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 鬼国母 - Title or role in a supernatural or mythological context, likely referring to a powerful female figure in an otherworldly setting.
  • 牙侩 - Archaic term referring to a middleman or intermediary in business, often associated with trade.
